title: The Opposite of a Glamping Yurt
summary:
Wanting to see Mt Hood and also knowing the weather was going to be chilly, we decided to book a night in a yurt at Lost Lake Campground and Lodge. Unfortunately for us, the view of Mt Hood at the lake was nonexistent, and the yurt was far from cozy. Nevertheless, we made the most of it and spent the afternoon stoking the fire and all playing games (lots of Splendor and Wingspan rounds) while it dumped rain. We spent a fitful night in the yurt, definitely colder and more uncomfortable than our van, but lucky for us, the next day we were headed to Vancouver, WA, to visit Colin's sister, where we knew we could shower, relax, and do some laundry.

-Helen

photos.caption: The view of Mt Hood from the Solera Brewery, where we stopped on the way to the yurt.
The cold and unglamourous yurt we stayed in.
